$schema: BJBS design tokens — grounded in css/style.css, js/theme-config.js, and observed HTML usage across index.html, about.html, crypto.html, contact.html, js/site.js, js/main.js

meta.mode: class-based dark mode via <html class="dark">, toggled in js/site.js, persisted to localStorage.theme

400.role: muted text, tab text (dark), pillar text (dark)

500.role: secondary body text, tab text (light), pillar text (light)

800.role: primary text (light-on-dark inverse), tab bg (dark), nav-item text (light)

900.role: true black, rarely used directly (body uses slate-900 ~ near-black via Tailwind, not pure black)

primary.role: primary action color: links, active tab, primary buttons (indigo-500 remapped to Apple blue)

primaryHover.role: hover state for primary blue (indigo-600 remap)

accent.role: Bitcoin-orange accent — eyebrow labels (text-btc), glow motif anchor color

accentDark.role: darker accent for dark-mode contrast needs (defined, currently unused in HTML)

glowGradientStops.description: 5-stop conic/linear gradient used ONLY for the decorative avatar ring (.glow-ring) and hero gradient text (.glow-text) — not a general palette, kept isolated to avoid bleeding into UI chrome

bgDark.role: page background, dark mode — note: slate-900 is remapped to #000000 by theme-config.js, so dark bg is pure black, not a soft dark gray

semantic.note: No dedicated success/warning/error/info tokens exist anywhere in the codebase today. Proposed additions below follow the Apple-system-color convention already implied by the blue/orange palette.

success.role: PROPOSED — form success states (e.g. subscribe confirmation currently uses plain text 'Subscribed ✓' with no color, js/main.js:88)

warning.role: PROPOSED — close to existing btc orange, reuse accent for warning to avoid adding a new hue

error.role: PROPOSED — form error / failed states (js/main.js:89 'Try again' currently unstyled)

fontFamily.source: js/theme-config.js:7; loaded via Google Fonts weights 400;500;600;700;800 (index.html:17)

scriptUnused.status: DEAD CODE — defined in css/style.css:124-128 but not referenced by any HTML file in the site. Either wire it up (e.g. for the pull-quote figure) or delete it.

spacing.cardPadding: p-6 (24px) standard card, p-8 (32px) for feature/quote cards

sm.usage: thumbnails, dropdown menu, venture cards (9 occurrences)

lg.usage: dominant card radius — glass cards, modals, pillar cards (62 occurrences — the de facto standard)

shadow.note: Only ONE shadow token exists (the glass surface). No separate elevation scale (sm/md/lg) for non-glass elements like the crypto-modal dialog, which currently ships with no shadow at all (crypto.html:56).

breakpoints.sm: 640px (Tailwind default; also the ONLY custom @media breakpoint in style.css, max-width:639px, css/style.css:190)
breakpoints.md: 768px (Tailwind default, used for md:px-12, md:text-5xl etc.)
breakpoints.lg: 1024px (Tailwind default, used for nav desktop/mobile split, js/site.js:60,66)

motion.durationDefault: Tailwind default `transition` ~150ms (used bareword 42 times across pages with no explicit duration — inconsistent)
motion.glassBtnEasing: 0.5s cubic-bezier(0.175, 0.885, 0.32, 1.6) — bouncy overshoot on hover scale, css/style.css:34

darkMode.strategy: Tailwind class strategy — 'dark' class added to <html> by default (index.html:2) and toggled by js/site.js:81-85, persisted via localStorage.theme
darkMode.defaultState: dark (every page ships with class="dark" hardcoded in the HTML tag; light mode is opt-out, not opt-in)
